source: camelid /v1/completions (logprobs=5) on the exact 2048-bucket prompt (Qwen3-4B-Q4_K_M, GPU-resident CUDA decode, temp 0/top_k 1/seed 0) — same runtime that produced context-parity-sweep-512-8192.json. Captured to make the sub-0.1-nat near-tie magnitude reader-verifiable rather than manifest-asserted.
interpretation: camelid's greedy pick (' llama', its top token) leads to correct fact recall; the llama.cpp oracle's pick (' the') is camelid's #2 by only 0.082 nat and drives the oracle into a degenerate 'the code is CMLD-2048' repetition loop. A benign sub-0.1-nat lower-precision-quant greedy near-tie, not a camelid decode error. Because the token stream is not identical to the oracle, the 2048 bucket is NOT promoted.
